Generic 'playful' language and cute designs alone do not establish toy intent without explicit evidence of play functionality.' } ``` **Detailed Classification Reasoning:**

STEP 0 - Foundational Definition Check: The rule explicitly warns: 'Do NOT hallucinate play intent. The product must have explicit evidence of play or toy purpose in its title, description, bullet points, category, or images.'

Evidence Analysis:

  • Primary function: Organizing notes and photos on magnetic surfaces (fridges, whiteboards)
  • Target users: 'families, offices and households' - mixed adult/professional use
  • Marketing language: 'Playful Learning' appears only in English title; French description emphasizes organization and decoration
  • No play mechanics: No interactive elements, games, or play scenarios described
  • Material: Resin magnets with integrated magnetic core - functional organizational tool

Why 'Playful Learning' is insufficient: The phrase is a generic marketing descriptor, not evidence of toy design. The product serves adult organizational needs (offices, households) equally to children. Cute bread/bagel designs do not constitute play intent.

Verdict: FP - Product is home organization/decoration, not a toy.
